title: Capsicum annuum (Cayenne)
intro: In a recent study conducted on Cayenne, it was found to contain potent capsaicin that is highly effective in reducing pain and inflammation. This herb contains essential nutrients such as Vitamin C, Vitamin K, Vitamin E, and minerals like potassium, iron, and magnesium. Capsule formulations, teas, tinctures, and topical creams are some of the commonly prepared Cayenne forms used for medicinal purposes. While taking Cayenne, one should be cautious as it may cause side effects such as nausea, indigestion, diarrhea, and skin irritation. Pregnant women and children should avoid taking Cayenne altogether, and individuals with a bleeding disorder, low blood pressure, or gastrointestinal problems should consult their doctor before using it.
